pgxxv-p1:
WE OF Alcoholics Anonymous believe that the reader will be interested in the medical estimate of the plan of recovery
described in this book. Convincing testimony must surely come from medical men who have had experience with the
sufferings of our members and have witnessed our return to health. A well-known doctor, chief physician at a nationally
prominent hospital specializing in alcoholic and drug addiction, gave Alcoholics Anonymous this letter:
pgxxv-p3-s1: I have specialized in the treatment of alcoholism for many years.
pgxxv-p3: I have specialized in the treatment of alcoholism for many years.
pgxxv-p4-s1: In late 1934 I attended a patient who, though he had been a competent businessman of good earning capacity, was an alcoholic of a type I had come to regard as hopeless.
pgxxv-p4: In late 1934 I attended a patient who, though he had been a competent businessman of good earning capacity, was an alcoholic of a type I had come to regard as hopeless.
pgxxv-p5-s1: In the course of his third treatment he acquired certain ideas concerning a possible means of recovery.
pgxxv-p5-s2: As part of his rehabilitation he commenced to present his conceptions to other alcoholics, impressing upon them that they must do likewise with still others.
pgxxv-p5-s3: This has become the basis of a rapidly growing fellowship of these men and their families.
pgxxv-p5-s4: This man and over one hundred others appear to have recovered.
pgxxv-p5: In the course of his third treatment he acquired certain ideas concerning a possible means of recovery. As part of his rehabilitation he commenced to present his conceptions to other alcoholics, impressing upon them that they must do likewise with still others. This has become the basis of a rapidly growing fellowship of these men and their families. This man and over one hundred others appear to have recovered.
pgxxv-p6-s1: I personally know scores of cases who were of the type with whom other methods had failed completely.
pgxxv-p6: I personally know scores of cases who were of the type with whom other methods had failed completely.
pgxxv-p7-s1: These facts appear to be of extreme medical importance; because of the extraordinary possibilities of rapid
